Rakhat   Zhussupekov                             10 .0 6 .23 Introduction The paper is a consulting project for the LLC “ Sarybay  TRD” and t he paper examines the managerial problem faced by the company  “ Sarybay  TRD ” ,  flour producer in the city of  Kostanay , and po tential solution to it. LLC «   Sarybay  TRD   »  is relatively new company and  operates in  Kazakhstan  for more  than 1 year  and does not have any novel growth strategies  to go beyond  the local market,  therefore company  needs  for expansion  geographically with the  main  aim of increasing its profits and gaining recognition in a foreign market . Therefore it is a managerial problem of the paper.  The company produces the three types of flour such as Top grade, I grade, II grade and bran. The relevance of the paper is that Kazakhstan  considered as a main flour producer and  Kostanay  where the company located is considered the best region for production the wheat in Kazakhstan.  The research goal of the present study   is  to define the  foreign  market for the entry by  Sarybay  TRD  and  identify  the entry strategy  for the chosen market . The objectives o f the study are: To give detailed description of the company and to identify managerial problem ; To use appropriate methodology for research  including macro- environment analysis, internal environment analy sis, selection of entry market a nd strategy development ; To analyze  countries for ente ring using specific criteria  and  come up with two  best  countries  choice ; To analyze micro- and  macroenvironments  of the two  selected  countries  for entry ; To  define   target  foreign market  for entry ; To analyze   the   competitive   position   of   the   company  and define  entry strategy  for the selected market .   Research Object and Subject .   The  research object  of this resear ch is company itself “ Sarybay  TRD ”  and the data is taken by conducting the  semi-structured interview with the eco nomist of the company . The  research subject  is the  foreign  market entry strategy. Description of the paper structure The   work consists of Introduction, Conclusion, 3 chapters .  The first part  of the bachelor thesis examines the overall overview of the company like products, the main suppliers, competitors,  managerial problem , methodology  and the use of instruments .  The second part  gives overall representation and analysis of the countries through  factors  and tools   for analyzing .  The third part  defines the  target  entry  market strategy for the company.   This is followed by conclusion which draws out the  target  country for entering and which entry market strategy should firm use. In order to achieve the goal the  primary source of data used in the form of   interview. It was conducted with the representative of the company in order to analyze the company and the market. Also, for the bachelor thesis paper the secondary sources of data were presented such as news, reports,  and data  from the company and in order to choose the best country different frameworks and models were analyzed through different factors.  For analysis of four countries the GAGE distance framework is used. CAGE (C-cultural,  A-administrative, G-geographic ,  E -economic) distance analysis is used for understanding the differences between countries and access the differences in terms of cultural, geographic, transportation and so on. Due to this analysis it was identified the two best countries and then to find to target market the other tools were used.  For example, PESTEL which stands for P-political, E-economic, S-social, T-technological, E-environmental,  L -legal was used in order to look at the  macroenvironment  of the countries and also it gives the set of potential opportunities and threats. Another instrument that was used is Porter’s Five Forces which helps to look at the competitive environment of the industry in terms of buyer’s bargaining power, threat of new entrants, industry rivalry, supplier’s bargaining power and threat of substitutes. The third instrument is Key Success Factors which  helps to understand what advantages and/or weak sides company has among competitors in the market. Another key instrument that was used is SWOT analysis where S-strengths, W-weaknesses, O-opportunities and T-threats. It is used for the systematization of the information that was collected for the chosen market in order to realize what opportunities and threats can be in the chosen market  and also to look at strengths and weaknesses the company has. For identification the  right entry strategy the OLI (O -ownership, L-location and I-internalization) paradigm model was used.  It gives the picture of three advantages like ownership, location and internalization and if all of them are pr esent the company can use FDI ( foreign direct investment). If something is not present the firm should use another entry strategy. As a result of all work the foreign market was chosen through different analyses and the entry strategy mode was defined for this market. The certain steps of how the entry  strategy for the foreign market were  described. CHAPTER 1 .   COMPANY AND THE MARKET, MANAGERIAL PROBLEM AND METHODOLOGY 1.1. World m arket of the flour  and flour market of Kazakhstan The  flour  produc tion industry is well-developed .   Cereals occupy an exclusive place in human nutrition: in some countries, they can account for up to 50% o f calories consumed. Among them  wheat, which, along with corn and rice, is key: about 30% of all   of sown areas for cereals accounted for by this crop  and at the same time, about 70%  wheat production is directed to food purposes, including for the production of flour . More than 600 million metric tons of wheat and corn flour are processed annually and consumed in the form of noodles, bread, pasta and other flour products around the world .   Flour is considered as one of the main need of the people because due to flour people can cook different things .  According to the Research   and   Markets the volume of consumption of wheat flour will raise to almost 411 million tons by 2024.  Kazakhstan is considered as one of main producer of wheat in the world.   Starting from 2002  Kazkahstan  is  in the top 6 of  wheat grain exporting countries.  Nowadays, Kazakhstan is in  top 10  exporting countries . The main countries  leaders in the export of wheat  are Russia, Canada, the United States, Argentina, France, Ukraine, Australia, India,  Kazakhstan .  These 9 countries account for almost 90% of world exports .   A gro-industrial complex  in Kazakhstan  is an in integral and one  of the important sector and part of the national economy  where products  which are significant  to society are produced and  where big  economic potential is concentrated .   For example, i n 2020, Kazakhstan produced 3,322.6 thousand tons of flour. It's almost 2% more than in 2019. It should be noted that flour is one of the main export products of Kazakhstan. According to the Ministry of Agriculture, for 4 months of the 2020-2021 season Kazakhstan exported 2 million 734 thousand tons of grain and flour. Big part was delivered to Central Asia. This demonstrates the  great potential of Kazakhstan  in this industry  and it worth to mention that  Kostanay  region where the company located is the main region of producing wheat because  Kostanay  is considered as the granary region .  Kostanay  region is on the first place in the country  on the production of flour (737 thousand tons ) . 1.4 .  M anagerial Problem Statement LLC « Sarybay  TRD » operates in  Kazakhstan  f or more than 1 year  and does not have any growth strategies  to go beyond its local  market ,   therefore  it needs to expand geographically with the  purpose  of increasing its profits and  getting  recognition in a foreign market . C ompany has capacity which allows  expanding  its production  abroad and the competition is high in the region where the firm operates .  Company is able to produce enough goods and it is a good period for company to go abroad.   Moreover, one of the  reasons  is the  wish  of the company’s management to understand own abilities and knowledge  to  extend  their business  abroad and not only work in local market .   The main challenge of  the thesis  paper is to  find  a new  foreign  market  where  the company can increase own potential of its activities and to  choose the   best  type of entry strategy for the entry  that will suit in  a new  market . 1.5 .  M ethods and tools for analysis The  right choice of methods and the appropriate study research  are  significant  steps in the research process.  When people use  wrong methodology  it  can  significantly  reflect on the quality of the  thesis paper  and show its  drawbacks  instead of its potential  benefits .  For getting better insights for analyzing the both sources like primary and secondary one will be used because it will give good understanding and it will be useful in this paper . Speaking about primary methods the author used a  semi-structured interview , i n order to gain in-depth information and certain insights of the company and industry in general, with  the representatives of LLC « Sarybay  TRD ». The questions of the  interviews can be found in the  a ppendix  1 .   The data such as  activities  of the company , range of products, financial indicators, and managerial pro blem proposal was obtained from leading economist  of the company. The  aim  of the interview  was to  obtain  the necessary  information  for a further analysis that would  help  to identify  different sides like  strong and weak of the  firm ,  the situation in the market .  The main reason of conduction interview with the economist is that this person knows the field and the some specifics of the industry  and aware of activities of the company .   The paper includes different secondary sources like articles, news, reports that can understand the topic deeper.  Also, articles about various frameworks that are used to show the difference between them and evaluate the environment ,  different changes over the time,   some  news  for assessing situation about policy, government regulations and so on .   Therefore, these  sources  give  the overall picture and characteristics that are connected to the industry. Choice of  instruments In order to choose  right  tool the company should evaluate its resource’s capabilities like value, rarity and not to be easily imitated . Also, to enter to foreign countries company should have the cluster of potential countries where it can enter and it can be done through assessing different factors like politics, economic ,  geograghic  factors and so on.  This analysis will be done through the CAGE distance analysis.  CAGE analysis is  stands for   C- Cultural,  A- Administrative,  G- Geographic and  E- Economic (CAGE)  and it is about  differences or distances between countries  that company should emphasize when going abroad.  It helps to understand the significant differences between countries and helps to estimate differences in spheres like culture, economy,  physical  distance and so on . After that when evaluating different countries, two of them can be considered as  the   best  ones. These two countries should be analyzed in depth  taking into account the factors that are related to the local market  o f  these countries  in general. In this  work , the  evaluation  of both macro- and microenvironment of markets will  b e  discussed . For evaluation  macroenvironment  different frameworks are used .  For instance, PESTEL analysis   and EFAS analysis. EFAS (External Factors Analysis Summary) framework in this case  can be considered as to  be i rrelevant because company does   not  have defined entry strategy on chosen market and to which  structure is best suited for this .   PESTEL analysis  technique  is the best choice of the analyzing the  ma croenvironment  because it is rather simple and practical for analyzing external factors of the company .   PESTEL is stands for P-politics, E-economics, S-social, T -technological, E-environmental and  L-legal.  In some cases PESTEL can be taken as PEST or  can be added ( for instance Ethical) .  PESTEL analysis can be an effective tool in the strategy for “ Sarybay  TRD”. In order to analyze competitors in the chosen markets the Porter’s 5 forces  or Value net  can be used.  The latter framework  is the analytical strategy tool also known as a  Coopetition Framework .  It  combines  two things like  strategy and game theory,  for  describ ing and analyzing  the behavior of  different  players wit hin a given industry or market .  At the same time, it includes only four main groups such as  cus tomers, suppliers, competitors and  complementors  that  have  an impact on  the course of any business .  For analyzing microenvironment  Porter's Five Forces  will be used.  Porter's Five Forces  is a model that identifies five competitive forces  that shape every industry and helps to determine an indu stry's weaknesses and strengths.  Also, it  helps in  as sessing   potential  adverse events that may somehow affect the business in future .  The five forces to Porter are: the threat of new entrants, bargaining power of customers (buyers), bargaining power of suppliers, threat of substitute products or services and rivalry among existing competitors . To compete in the chosen market w hen  company entering to the new market  it should have   to  analyze  Key Success F actors ( KSF)  in order to have a view that it c an compete in its target market.   Understanding success factors is an integral part of any management position to  create and execute a  working  business plan .  When the suitable  market  is chosen, the  business  performance of “ Sarybay  TRD” should  be assessed  due to  the use of  such  tool . To analyze the chosen market  the primary SWOT analysis will be used.  SWOT is stands for Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities and Threats.  When the firm conducts an external analysis it identifies its threats and opportunities. Also, it gives the overall picture how the competition in this industry will evolve and what this evolution has for threats and opportunities. While analyzing the external analysis that focused on the threats and opportunities, the internal analysis gives the organization the picture of its own  strengths  and weaknesses. Moreover, it helps the firm to identify the competitive advantages that can be the sources and capabilities of the company .   Based on this tool the company can choose the suitable strategy.  In addition, SOAR analysis can be an alternative to the SWOT. The SOAR analysis supports the Strengths and  Opportunities  of the SWOT analysis but  has  another points  instead of Weaknesses and Threats like  the Aspirations and Results.   Aspirations is  about  what the firm who they want  to serve, wants to do  and where they wish to operate. Speaking about the results section is about  how the  company  will  recognize  and track their progress toward their Aspirations and Opportunities . However, this paper will rely on the SWOT analysis because it is more analytical approach. In or der to help company evaluate  the competitive advantages and find appropriate entry strategy the  OLI paradigm model will be used.  OLI is an acronym for ownership,  location and localization advantages of   the company on a   potential market .  It will help to identify the entry strategy. Another framework that can be used instead of OLI model is LLL ( linkage-leverage-learning ) tool that was developed by Mathews J.A .  It is a strategic framework that  focused on accelerated internationalization—creating a light, flexible lattice structure .  However, this framework is used for emerging companies .   The last evaluation of the suggested strategy  Business Model Canva s  or Value Creation Chain can be used. This paper will use the former model  because  it gives overall picture and  understanding  what changes should be made when the company entering new market and  understanding of the business model where  the company  can get insights about customers that serve about what value propositions are offered so on . 1.6 . The main challenge of the paper is  to  find  a new  foreign  market  where the company can increase own potential of its activities and to  choose the  best  type of entry strategy for the entry  that will suit in a new market. Russia Cultural Speaking about Russia the author can say that it has different religion and different customs. Concerning language aspect most Kazakh people can speak in Russian but not everyone. Also, there are some differences in values, but in the value like hospitality the difference is not significant it is almost the same. By criteria like level of life it can be seen that cultures are similar. Moreover, there was a survey which was conducted by  Bendas  T.V. In this survey there were 50 Kazakhs and 50 Russians and different methods were used  ( experiment , test  Kettela , MMPI and others). Based on the results it can be seen that  Russians surpassed Kazakhs in terms of characteristics such as emotional stability; extraversion and social boldness; self-confidence and self-esteem, intellectual activity and productivity; working capacity; social needs and social significance; altruism; conformism; daydreaming; teaching and administrati ve capacity. The difference of  Kazakhs from Russians was observed in terms of: orientation towards official subordination; ethnocentrism (this was given only in communication with representatives of other ethical groups, and not one's own); collectivism: anxiety and self-doubt; creativity and artistry: higher affiliation motivation (belonging to a group) . Administrative Also, now from political side Kazakhstan and Russia share a long-standing and complex relationship that is shaped by their historical ties, geographic proximity, and strategic interests. Historically, Kazakhstan was part of the Soviet Union, and its economy, political system, and culture were closely intertwined with Russia's. After the collapse of the Soviet Union, Kazakhstan became an independent country, but it maintained close ties with Russia through various agreements and partnerships. Also, Kazakhstan and Russia are both the part of different organizations such as UN, OSCE, CIS, CSTO, SCO and other international organizations . Today, due to some political situation in the world some   countries   have   been   opposing   Russia and it makes the relationship with Russia unstable.  In 2022, due to recent situation it can be seen  that Kazakhstan almost   does not hide that it does not support Russia in the conflict situation with Ukraine . The currency exchange is rather high, for example, 1 rub is equal to 6.7  tenge . In addition, speaking about the taxation of Russia is the following: Corporate Tax – 20%; VAT – 20%; agreement on the avoidance of double taxation – yes; but for the socially significant food and non-food products VAT is charged at the rate of  10% ; import tax – 10% . Geographic The distance to the capital from  Kostanay  warehouse is 2050 km and the transportation cost is equal to 119 583 RUB. Moreover, Russia is the biggest country in the world and the population of Russia is 145, 5 million. In Russia, landscape varies from desert to frozen coastline, high mountains to big marshes. The area of Russia is 17,098,242 km 2 .  Also, the climate of Russia is quite diverse. Russia has a varied climate, e.g. arctic, subtropical, temperate,  subarctic .  Russia has rich soils that can provide a high yield of wheat. Most of the land used to grow wheat in Russia is in the  chernozem  zone, one of the most fertile soils in the world. This makes it possible to obtain high-quality grain, which makes it competitive on world markets . Thanks to favorable climatic conditions and large areas of agricultural land, Russia ranks first among the countries of the world in wheat exports. It follows that Russia as a whole does not need imported wheat, since it fully covers domestic needs for wheat and flour . Economic Regarding the economic situation the author can say that Russia has a lot of natural resources and resources for producing the wheat and flour. Gray market  in Kyrgyzstan  (possibility of the  fake Kazakh flour ) The threat that can appear is that Kyrgyz firms can start to  fake Kazakh flour . There  was  a situation in  Kyrgyzstan and in  Uzbekistan when Uzbek  and Kyrgyz  firms started to  fake Kazakh flour  in order to show that it is Kazakh flour. The thing is that the  Kazakh wheat is better  compare to the  Uzbek  and Kyrgyz  one s  and in order to have an advantage in terms of the quality of the flour Uzbek  and Kyrgyz  firms  fake Kazakh flour  but in reality it is the Uzbek  and Kyrgyz  flour .  As a result, this might happen in the Kyrgyzstan  again  that can lead to the less popularity of the Kazakh flour in the market. The risk of repeated restrictions on flour exports by the Kazakh authorities due to the unstable political situation in the country   There is a threat from the government of Kazakhstan to the prohibition of the export of flour at the legislative level. There was a case in 2022 when government  of Kazakhstan put the restriction to the export of goods from Kazakhstan. It was introduced from July 8 to September 30, 2022. The restriction was removed on the export of grain and flour from 10 th  of September . Therefore, such a factor can be considered as a threat because it will lead to the loss of opportunity and lost profit. After analyzing SWOT it can be concluded that this analysis gave the information that was collected for the chosen market in order to realize what opportunities and threats can be in the Kyrgyz market and also to look at strengths and weaknesses the company has.  “ Sarybay  TRD” has strengths in terms of the price, assortment, access to the raw materials and having a strong brand of Kazakh flour which give the company  great benefits  among competitors. However, the company has a weak marketing and does not have  any  international experience. Speaking about opportunities  due to the fact that the population in Kyrgyzstan is increasing, it results in high demand for the flour because  flour  considered as of one of the main products in human life and Kyrgyzstan is one of the members of EAEU, which gives some opportunity in terms of the tariffs and so on. All of these opportunities give a great potential for the company in the market. Concerning threats it can be concluded that there are  some situations  like gray market in Kyrgyzstan, aggravation of conflict between Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan, and possibility  risk of repeated restrictions on flour exports by the Kazakh authorities  which can effect on the market and lead to the less popularity of the Kazakh flour. 3.2 .  F orm of entry As it was mentioned previously LLC “ Sarybay  TRD” wants to expand own business  there are  several reasons including the desire of management to realize own skills and  knowledge  for expansion, to gain profit and that the company has the capacity to produce the product.  Also, it includes market seeking where Kyrgyz market is chosen by sales potential that suggests for the company . For the identification the right entry strategy for the company t he OLI ( ownership, location and internalization)  framework  is used.  When company has all three advantages like ownership, location and internalization the firm can implement Foreign Direct Investment (FDI). If one or more of these benefits are not present the company needs to use another entry strategy.  For example,  according to this model (see the figure below)  that was created by Dunning,  if the company does not have the ownership advantage it should stay at home, if the firm does not have the location benefit it should use export as an entry strategy and if there is no internalization advantage the company should use the licensing/contracting . The OLI model Ownership advantage .  The company needs such benefit in order to overcome the  liability of foreignness . “ Sarybay  TRD” has the following advantages:   No language barrier .  Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan speak almost with the same language. Also, in both countries people can speak in Russian and it will help to have good negotiations.  Economies of scale .  The company has enough capacity for producing flour making it possible to expand abroad. Access to the raw materials .  Kazakhstan’s wheat compare to the Kyrgyz one is better in terms of the g luten .  Also, the territory of Kazakhstan allows  to produce  more wheat and the climate is more favorable for this. Having a strong brand of Kazakh flour.  As it was mention Kazakh flour is considered  as one best flour . Thanks to the certain p roperties  ( like  high-quality grain with a protein cont ent of 14% and gluten from 28%) , flour made from Kazakh wheat, especially  Kostanay  wheat, is highly valued and in great demand among the countries of Central Asia . Location  advantage .   Advantages  ( or disadvantages)  that include,  for  instance ,  labour  costs, an efficient and skilled  labour  force, tariffs, transport costs or natural resources. Labor force .  In Kyrgyzstan there is a low labor cost compare to Kazakhstan. For example, in Kyrgyzstan the minimum wage for 2022 is 22.565 USD  while in Kazakhstan in the same period the minimum wage is 139 USD . Transportation by land.  The transportation to Kyrgyzstan can be done through trains but in order to look at the performance of the company in the market  and evaluate  will  it  be profitable or not, transportation will be done by land. No access to the cheap raw materials.  I n Kyrgyzstan, as it was mentioned, the wheat is not good enough compare to the Kazakh one and that is why Kyrgyzstan imports wheat and flour.  For the company it will be too disadvantageous to use th e raw materials from Kyrgyzstan and because of it will be too costly for the company to order from abroad and also it has some transportations and legal risks.  Internalization .   Speaking about internalization it can be seen that the company is not big enough and cannot allow a lot of capital investments because it can lose its competitive advantage and profits.  A s  a result, company cannot build a flour mill in Kyrgyzstan.  In addition,  Sarybay  TRD does not have any experience  or  already established relations  in Kyrgyzstan which make it hard for the company to do activities in the foreign market.   Analyzing factors and it can be said that the company does not have a lot of location advantages and because of the lack of international experience and capital investment it concluded that the best entry strategy for the company is  export .   Moreover, the company has a low-cost strategy meaning that export will not require high costs such as cons truction a new production mill. 3.2.1 .  Export as an entry strategy For successful implementation of the export as an entry strategy the company will do the some steps. They are:  Find a client; Often, the wholesale buyer himself finds the  Sarybay  TRD and contacts him by e-mail or by phone (representatives of Kyrgyz companies are located in  Kostanay  and constantly monitor the market in search of suppliers). Submit the necessary documents to obtain certi ficates for products for export; There is a certificate of origin, which is called form ST-1. This document certifies that the goods originate from a member country of the CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States). This certificate of origin is issued to obtain tariff preferences in the country of destination (reduction or exemption from customs duties), non-tariff regulation (bans, restrictions on import/export of goods) . Kazakhstan and Kyrgyzstan are the members of the  CIS.  Also, it is needed to get a phytosanitary certificate. This certificate to be prepared when supplying goods of plant origin to other countries and confirms that the products have passed phytosanitary control in the state body . Sign the contract and specifications, with the amount of the cont ract, terms, terms of delivery; Since the companies are located in different countries, initial negotiations will be held with representatives located in  Kostanay , or by phone, if the client contacts the advertisement posted on the notice board of the agricultural direction. After the parties have discussed all the clauses of the contract orally, the contract is concluded on paper. To do this, first one party signs two  copies  of the contract and sends scanned copies to the partner, then the second party signs and also sends scanned copies back.  Immediately after that, the original contracts in two copies are sent by letter through a courier service  and  then the second party sends back the fully signed contract. Receive an advance pa yment (100% of the total cost); According to the terms of the contract,  Sarybay  TRD LLP ships the goods only after 100% prepayment. Since Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan are members o f the EAEU , customs clearance takes place in a simplified mode ; S end the flour to the destina tion specified in the contract; Since, according to the contract, the terms of delivery will be FCA - "Free Carrier", in which the goods are transferred to the carrier appointed by the buyer, the seller assumes no obligations and expenses for transportation. All necessary accounting documents will be sent with the car.  Then there is control over the return of these documents with signatures and seals of counterparties 3.2.2 .  Business model can vas adaptation There will be some changes in the business model of “ Sarybay  TRD” because of the entry to the Kyrgyz market. Customer segments It will be not changed a lot. It will be a wholesale buyer  who will be interested in the product and sell it in the Kyrgyz market.  It is a B2C segment since this wholesale buyer is a part of distribution channel to the end customer. Value proposition “ Sar ybay  TRD” will expand own products to the Kyrgyz market  with own products  and expand all assortment to the market.   It will begin with the large volume of flour, 20 tons per shipment. Channels In the form of promotion the company use s  direct marketing because it  is  more convenient for the company since it does not imply a lot of costs  that can influence on the final price of the product.   Another marketing strategies  the company decide d  not to apply.  Customer relations The customer relation would be the same. The company does not need in the additional specialist (sale manager) who will be well familiar with the market. The sales department will be responsible for that. Revenue streams  I t  will be the same since the company gets the payment according to the contract.   And gets the full payment.  The payment is done in terms of transaction of money. Key resources The resources of the c ompany are going to be the same. In the human resource the company will not hire a new manager that will work with Kyrgyz market.  In general, all resources are going to be the same. Key activities  Speaking about activities they will be  not  expanded  a lot , for example,  the  production volume is not changed immediately, and it will change gradually.    The control over the Kyrgyz marker will not be needed because the new client of the company will be responsible for the further  retail sales of products in the market. Key partners The company is going to order materials from its suppliers so it is not being changed.  Also,  about transportation services “ Sarybay  TRD” will not be responsible for this and the buyer will take this responsibility for the transportation  to the foreign market . Cost structure The cost structure will change a little bit: Registration fees for   the export like  the certificate of  p hytosanitary  and certificate of  origin Slight rise in production costs Transportation costs will not be  changed and  considered since as it was mentioned the company will not be responsible for this cost, the buyer will take this responsibility. Conclusions In the chapter 3 it was identified the competitive strategy of   the company  “ Sarybay  TRD” and the SWOT analysis was done. The strengths and weaknesses of the company were analyzed   and possible opportunities and threats that can appear in the Kyrgyz market were defined. Due to the OLI model it was defined that the export will be  used as entry strategy mode for the company. The main steps of how the export strategy were described and  In the Business Model Canvas the main changes were highlighted but there were little changes  since the company will not have a lot of costs connected to the export .   Conclusion The consulting project for the LLC “ Sarybay  TRD” was conducted.  LLC “ Sarybay  TRD ”  has great potential for development and expansion of own business to the foreign market which was analyzed through the different analyses and own structure,  frameworks  and so on .  The firm decided to enter to one of the four CIS countries which were analyzed. The first step was to identify the two best countries out of four through different factors  with the help of CAGE distance analysis  and they are Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan. Then, there was a deep analysis of both countries in terms of the external and competitive environments. After the analysis it was determined that the Kyrgyz market is favorable for the entry.  In terms of the  macroenvironment  Kyrgyzstan is better compare to Uzbekistan since there is less technological development in the industry and the country imports the Kazakh flour which has a good reputation in the market. The flour is the essential product in human life and due to increasing of population in the country the flour will be always in demand. After analyzing the external environment it was concluded that Kyrgyzstan has more potential for the company to enter and doing business.  The Kyrgyz market has some disadvantages in terms of the competitive environment like capital investments, differentiation of the product is low,  however ,  there are some good points such as low switching for buyers, having a strong brand of Kazakh flour  and others .  Moreover, when the company entering to the new market it will preserve own competitive benefits in terms of economies of scale and therefore there will be low price in the market, and “ Sarybay  TRD” has more assortment of wheat flour than some of the companies in Kyrgyzstan, and the company has the direct ac cess to the cheap raw materials which also indicates as an advantages among competitors in the Kyrgyz market. Analyzing the form of entry strategy it was identified the entry strategy for the company is export since it does not require  a lot of investme nts.  The certain steps were provided as well of how the export entry strategy will be done  to the foreign market . Concerning the company’s activities it will not change dramatically. The main changes will be that new client appear  ( wholesale buyer)  and also it will be needed to get documents for export such as certificate of origin  and phytosanitary  certificate .  The production will be increased but not immediately because company has enough capacity and it will increase gradually. The paper reached its goal of finding the best market for the company in order to expand the business abroad and the best strategy for entry.  The paper provided the detailed company’s description and its competitive nes s.  The frameworks that  were  used in this paper are CAGE  distance analysis, PESTEL, Porter’s  Five  forces, Key Success Factors, SWOT analysis, OLI model and Business Model Canvas. The author believes that the results of the  thesis  paper can help the co mpany in its future development.   Referenc e list Agro export (2022)   Wheat flour.
